facts.text: Asia is the largest and most populous continent on Earth.
The Himalayas, including Mount Everest, form the highest mountain range in the world.
Asia contains enormous deserts, rainforests, river basins, steppes, island chains, and megacities.
China and India, both located in Asia, are among the most populous countries in the world.
The continent includes major rivers such as the Yangtze, Ganges, Mekong, Indus, and Tigris-Euphrates systems.
Asia is home to major world religions including Hinduism, Buddhism, Islam, Christianity, Judaism, Sikhism, and more.
The monsoon climate plays a major role in agriculture and daily life across large parts of South and Southeast Asia.
Tokyo, Delhi, Shanghai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Bangkok are among the continent's vast urban centers.
Asia includes both the Arabian Peninsula and the island nations of Japan, Indonesia, and the Philippines.
The Silk Road connected many parts of Asia with Europe and Africa through trade, culture, and ideas.
Rice is a staple food across much of Asia, but diets vary enormously from region to region.
Asia's history includes some of the world's oldest civilizations, empires, and literary traditions.
Because the continent is so large, Asia contains nearly every climate type from arctic cold to tropical heat.
Modern Asia plays a massive role in global manufacturing, finance, technology, cinema, and culture.
The Himalayas are the highest mountain range on Earth and stretch across several Asian countries.
They include Mount Everest and shape climate, rivers, and cultures across a huge part of Asia.
Himalayas is one of the most recognizable places or topics associated with Asia.
Travel guides about Asia frequently feature Himalayas.
Learning about Himalayas helps explain part of the geography, culture, or history of Asia.
For many visitors, Himalayas stands out as a major highlight of Asia.
Maps, documentaries, and guidebooks about Asia often highlight Himalayas.
The story of Himalayas is closely tied to how many people understand Asia.
People around the world often associate Asia with places or traditions like Himalayas.
Himalayas is frequently mentioned when describing the diversity and appeal of Asia.
One reason Asia feels so memorable to travelers is the presence of places such as Himalayas.
From tourism to history, Himalayas plays a notable role in how Asia is presented to the world.
